pattern: Row-by-row dynamic programming with left and right prefix maxima folds the absolute-distance penalty into linear-time transitions.
hints: Process rows top to bottom, keeping the best achievable score ending in each column.
The penalty abs(c1-c2) can be split: from the left the cost grows with column, from the right it shrinks, so two sweeps suffice.
Maintain best[c] = dp[c] - c going left-to-right and dp[c] + c going right-to-left, then add the current cell's value.

edge_inputs.description: penalty makes staying in place better than chasing a high value

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(2, size))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(2, size))
    while m * n > 4000:
        n = max(1, n // 2)
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
